text.title: Blurred Roles and Team Evolution Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding Blurred Roles and Team Evolution plus the universal skip/run routing pair (referenced by the decision tree).

rule.statement: Every Venn-overlap zone (PM∩Design, Design∩Eng, PM∩Eng) MUST have exactly one human DRI; agents may fill the zone but never own the decision.
rule.rationale: Without a single DRI per zone, parallel role-persona agents produce contradictions and the team ships the loudest recommendation, not the right one.

rule.statement: PMs MUST hold working fluency in design, data, model behavior, distribution, and pricing — not just spec writing and roadmap ownership.
rule.rationale: As AI scales engineering throughput, the bottleneck shifts to discovery, evaluation, and judgment; PMs without adjacent fluency become the constraint.

rule.statement: Every 'PM speaks design' expectation MUST be paired with a symmetric expectation on the design side (e.g., designers track metrics); one-sided blurring is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Asymmetric blurring is extractive, not collaborative; one side ends up doing two jobs while the other guards specialism.

rule.statement: Every deep domain (security, ML eval, accessibility) MUST retain at least one depth specialist; full-blurring in these domains is forbidden.
rule.rationale: T-shaped fluency scales; eliminating specialisation creates a team that is mediocre everywhere and expert nowhere.

rule.statement: Role overlap MUST be re-audited quarterly using commit/PR-author distribution (git shortlog by file area) as the truth signal — not self-reported descriptions.
rule.rationale: Self-reported role splits are systematically wrong by ~30%; git shortlog surfaces who actually touches which areas.

rule.statement: The 2:1 PM:Engineer ratio (Ng 2024) MUST be treated as a per-team hypothesis to measure, not a universal target to copy.
rule.rationale: The inversion only holds when engineers are heavily AI-augmented; treating it as a target leads to over-hiring PMs and under-building.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` do not ALL hold OR any disqualifier in `## Skip If` fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and document the skip reason in the cycle artefact.
rule.rationale: A minimum-form decision tree must route 'preconditions-not-satisfied' to skip; this rule is the canonical target.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` ALL hold AND no disqualifier fires, the agent MUST run the methodology end-to-end and produce the artefact specified in 02-output-contract.xml.
rule.rationale: Pair to skip-this-methodology; together they form the always-present minimal route.
